kind: StatefulSet
metadata:

  labels:

    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  resourceVersion: "161960"

spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    spec:
      affinity:
        podAntiAffinity:
          preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - podAffinityTerm:
              labelSelector:
                matchLabels:
                  app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
                  app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
              topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
            weight: 100
      containers:
      - command:
        - /cockroach/cockroach
        - start
        - --insecure
        - --advertise-addr=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
        - --http-addr=0.0.0.0
        - --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
        - --cache=25%
        - --max-sql-memory=25%

            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: COCKROACH_CHANNEL
          value: kubernetes-insecure
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: cockroachdb
        ports:
        - containerPort: 26257
          name: grpc
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 6
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
          name: datadir
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: crdb-cluster-sa
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
  updateStrategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
    type: RollingUpdate
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - apiVersion: v1
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
      volumeMode: Filesystem
    status:
      phase: Pending

content.thinking:
The logs tell me quite a bit:

1. The pods are all running (3/3 Ready)
2. The join flags look correct - they're using the headless service DNS names
3. `crdb-cluster-0` log shows: `status: initialized new cluster` and `clusterID: 45c71578-6b51-4373-8a89-f68e2fc25737` and `nodeID: 1` - this means crdb-cluster-0 has already initialized its own cluster!

But the issue is that the WARNING says it can't contact the other nodes. Wait, let me re-read the log output...
The warning about being unable to contact other nodes combined with the `initialized new cluster` status suggests that crdb-cluster-0 bootstrapped itself as a single-node cluster instead of waiting for the others to join. Even though all three pods are running with the correct `--join` flags pointing to each other, something went wrong with the initialization sequence.

Let me check what the other pods are showing in their logs to understand the full picture.

content.text: The config looks correct — `--join` and `--advertise-addr` are properly set using the headless service DNS. But pod-0 shows it already initialized itself. Let me check the other pods.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb logs crdb-cluster-1 2>&1 | tail -20
kubectl -n cockroachdb logs crdb-cluster-2 2>&1 | tail -20
input.description: Check logs for cluster-1 and cluster-2
